code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to clean numeric objects (e.g., remove trailing .0)
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        # Remove surrounding quotes and format numbers with commas
        obj = o.strip('"')
        # Try to format numeric values
        try:
            # Handle integers and floats
            num = float(obj)
            if num.is_integer():
                return f"{int(num):,}"
            else:
                # Keep up to two decimal places, strip trailing zeros
                formatted = f"{num:,.2f}".rstrip('0').rstrip('.')
                return formatted
        except ValueError:
            return obj

    # Convert ordinal prefixes like "1st", "2nd" to words
    def _ordinal_word(ord_str: str) -> str:
        mapping = {
            "1st": "first",
            "2nd": "second",
            "3rd": "third",
            "4th": "fourth",
            "5th": "fifth",
            "6th": "sixth",
            "7th": "seventh",
            "8th": "eighth",
            "9th": "ninth",
            "10th": "tenth",
        }
        return mapping.get(ord_str, ord_str)

    # More natural templates; some include the subject directly
    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The key is the predicate name; the value is a format string that can be
    # interpolated with the cleaned object via the placeholder {obj}.
    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "city": "is located in the city of {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "runwayLength": "The runway length of {subject} is {obj}",
        "runwayName": "{obj} is the runway name of {subject}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is located in the country of {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "The ICAO Location Identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "The IATA location identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "The IATA location identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "the length of the first runway is {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "the length of the first runway is {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "the first runway number is {obj}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the second runway is {obj}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the third runway is {obj}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "the length of the fourth runway is {obj} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the fourth runway is {obj}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "the fifth runway number is {obj}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the fifth runway is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "has its hub at {obj}",
        # General predicates
        "language": "The {obj} are spoken in {subject}",
        "officialLanguage": "The official language of {subject} is {obj}",
        "headquarter": "The headquarters of {subject} are located in {obj}",
        "headquarters": "The headquarters of {subject} are located in {obj}",
        "leader": "The leader of {subject} is {obj}",
        "owner": "The owner of {subject} is {obj}",
        # Revised templates for better natural order
        "capital": "{obj} is the capital of {subject}",
        "mayor": "{obj} is the mayor of {subject}",
        "leader": "{obj} is the leader of {subject}",
        # Entity classification predicates (class, order, division, etc.)
        "class": "{subject} class is {obj}",
        "order": "{subject} order is {obj}",
        "division": "{subject} division is {obj}",
        "family": "{subject} family is {obj}",
        # Fallback generic template
        "default": "{obj} is the {predicate} of {subject}"
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            obj = _clean_obj(t.object)
            # Special handling for runway surface type predicates (e.g., 1stRunwaySurfaceType)
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
                # Extract ordinal prefix (e.g., "1st", "2nd", "3rd", "4th", "5th")
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwaySurfaceType", "")
                # Human‑readable phrase
                phrase = f"the surface of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj}"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            # Special handling for runway length predicates (e.g., 1stRunwayLengthFeet, 4thRunwayLengthFeet)
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwayLengthFeet", "")
                phrase = f"the length of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj} feet"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwayLengthMetre", "")
                phrase = f"the length of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj} metres"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                # If the template already includes the subject, render it directly
                if "{subject}" in tmpl:
                    sentences.append(tmpl.format(subject=subject, obj=obj) + ".")
                else:
                    parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine remaining parts (those without full‑sentence templates) into one sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join all generated sentences; if there is only one, return it without extra spaces
    return " ".join(sentences).strip()

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to clean numeric objects (e.g., remove trailing .0)
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        # Remove surrounding quotes and format numbers with commas
        obj = o.strip('"')
        # Try to format numeric values
        try:
            # Handle integers and floats
            num = float(obj)
            if num.is_integer():
                return f"{int(num):,}"
            else:
                # Keep up to two decimal places, strip trailing zeros
                formatted = f"{num:,.2f}".rstrip('0').rstrip('.')
                return formatted
        except ValueError:
            return obj

    # Convert ordinal prefixes like "1st", "2nd" to words
    def _ordinal_word(ord_str: str) -> str:
        mapping = {
            "1st": "first",
            "2nd": "second",
            "3rd": "third",
            "4th": "fourth",
            "5th": "fifth",
            "6th": "sixth",
            "7th": "seventh",
            "8th": "eighth",
            "9th": "ninth",
            "10th": "tenth",
        }
        return mapping.get(ord_str, ord_str)

    # More natural templates; some include the subject directly
    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The key is the predicate name; the value is a format string that can be
    # interpolated with the cleaned object via the placeholder {obj}.
    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "city": "is located in the city of {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "runwayLength": "The runway length of {subject} is {obj}",
        "runwayName": "{obj} is the runway name of {subject}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is located in the country of {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "The ICAO Location Identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "The IATA location identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "The IATA location identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "the length of the first runway is {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "the length of the first runway is {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "the first runway number is {obj}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the second runway is {obj}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the third runway is {obj}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "the length of the fourth runway is {obj} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the fourth runway is {obj}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "the fifth runway number is {obj}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the fifth runway is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "has its hub at {obj}",
        # General predicates
        "language": "The {obj} are spoken in {subject}",
        "officialLanguage": "The official language of {subject} is {obj}",
        "headquarter": "The headquarters of {subject} are located in {obj}",
        "headquarters": "The headquarters of {subject} are located in {obj}",
        "leader": "The leader of {subject} is {obj}",
        "owner": "The owner of {subject} is {obj}",
        "capital": "The capital of {subject} is {obj}",
        # Entity classification predicates (class, order, division, etc.)
        "class": "{subject} class is {obj}",
        "order": "{subject} order is {obj}",
        "division": "{subject} division is {obj}",
        "family": "{subject} family is {obj}",
        # Fallback generic template
        "default": "{obj} is the {predicate} of {subject}"
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            obj = _clean_obj(t.object)
            # Special handling for runway surface type predicates (e.g., 1stRunwaySurfaceType)
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
                # Extract ordinal prefix (e.g., "1st", "2nd", "3rd", "4th", "5th")
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwaySurfaceType", "")
                # Human‑readable phrase
                phrase = f"the surface of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj}"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            # Special handling for runway length predicates (e.g., 1stRunwayLengthFeet, 4thRunwayLengthFeet)
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwayLengthFeet", "")
                phrase = f"the length of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj} feet"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):

                phrase = f"the length of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj} metres"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                # If the template already includes the subject, render it directly
                if "{subject}" in tmpl:
                    sentences.append(tmpl.format(subject=subject, obj=obj) + ".")
                else:
                    parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine remaining parts (those without full‑sentence templates) into one sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join all generated sentences; if there is only one, return it without extra spaces
    return " ".join(sentences).strip()

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
